{"step_by_step_thinking": "The patient presents with brown-colored urine, oliguria, mild facial puffiness, pedal edema, 3+ proteinuria, and 100 RBCs/hpf with granular casts. Minimal change disease, membranous glomerulonephritis, and FSGS can all present with these findings. However, IgA nephropathy typically presents with hematuria following an upper respiratory tract infection and does not usually present with proteinuria in the nephrotic range. Therefore, IgA nephropathy is the least likely to present with this combination of findings.", "answer_choice": "D"}
